title: Vowel or Consonant

content.problemStatement: Write a program that takes a lowercase English alphabet character as input and uses a 'switch' statement to determine if it's a **Vowel** or **Consonant**.
constraints: Each character is a lowercase English alphabet letter
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single lowercase English alphabet character.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print "Vowel" if the character is a vowel (a, e, i, o, u) otherwise print "Consonant". Each answer on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The character 'i' is a vowel, so the program prints 'Vowel'.
The character 't' is not a vowel, therefore the output is 'Consonant'.
The character 'a' belongs to the vowel set, so the program prints 'Vowel'.

solution.approach: The problem only requires checking whether a given character belongs to the fixed set of vowels. Using a switch (or a set lookup) gives O(1) time per character. We read T, then for each test case read the character, apply the switch, and output the corresponding word. The algorithm is linear in the number of test cases and uses constant extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) total, O(1) per test case.
